findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Gap
findings.description: LearnPath has significant WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility gaps, which is a critical weakness compared to EduAccess LMS, which is fully compliant and has achieved WCAG 2.2 AA conformance. This compliance is increasingly becoming a deciding factor for government contracts and institutional adoption.
assumptions: Accessibility compliance is a key factor for customers, especially in government contracts.

evidence.excerpt: EduAccess LMS... WCAG 2.1 AA fully conformant... achieved WCAG 2.2 AA conformance in Q3 2025.

recommendations: Prioritize accessibility improvements to meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

findings.title: Competitive Pricing Strategy
findings.description: LearnPath's pricing at $8/user/month is competitive compared to EduAccess LMS at $12/user/month. However, the pricing strategy may be perceived as low, which could impact perceived value and investment in product development.
assumptions: Lower pricing may lead to perceptions of lower quality or fewer features.
recommendations: Consider a value-based pricing strategy that reflects the quality and features offered.

findings.title: Emerging Accessibility Threats
findings.description: Competitor B, SkillForge, is facing backlash due to similar accessibility gaps and has lost a significant government deal. This indicates a market trend where accessibility compliance is becoming a critical factor for success, posing a risk to LearnPath if improvements are not made.
assumptions: Accessibility compliance will continue to be a decisive factor in contract awards.

evidence.excerpt: Lost a $600K government deal last quarter due to accessibility non-compliance.

recommendations: Accelerate the development of accessibility features to avoid losing market share.

findings.title: Leverage Accessibility as a Differentiator
findings.description: Given the significant accessibility gaps in LearnPath, there is an opportunity to position the product as a leader in accessibility by committing to WCAG 2.1 AA compliance and promoting this commitment in marketing efforts.
assumptions: There is a growing market demand for accessible learning solutions.

evidence.excerpt: Achieved WCAG 2.2 AA conformance... won 3 major government contracts citing accessibility.

recommendations: Develop a marketing strategy that highlights accessibility improvements and compliance.

summary: LearnPath faces critical accessibility compliance gaps compared to competitors, particularly EduAccess LMS, which poses a significant risk in a market increasingly valuing accessibility. There are opportunities to leverage accessibility improvements as a key differentiator while addressing pricing perceptions to enhance market positioning.
